title: coupon — authorization policies
description: Two policies, one per owned entity. Admin-heavy for writes — marketing/admin/owner author coupons, only admin/owner can reverse a redemption (financial reversal — audit-critical). Every ability defers to spatie/laravel-permission for the permission check; scope narrowing runs via the ResolveScope middleware (tenant context; no branch scoping for coupons — they are tenant-wide by design).

abilities.scope: marketing + admin + owner. Refuses discount_type / discount_amount edits once any redemption exists (returns COUPON_TERMS_LOCKED)
admin + owner only. Soft-delete restricted to inactive coupons with zero non-reversed redemptions
marketing + admin + owner. Refuses when coupon is already deactivated + valid_until is past
admin + owner + support only. Confidential financial fields (discount_amount, usage_cap, minimum_order_amount) are redacted for readers without this permission.

marketing + admin + owner + support. Customers see their own redemptions through the finance::invoice ledger (never via a /me/coupon-redemptions route — coupons are never surfaced independently to end users; they appear as discount line items on invoices).
admin + owner only. Financial reversal — audit-critical. Every reverse fires CouponRedemptionReversed + notifies Finance module (Wave 4) to adjust the underlying invoice line.
admin + owner only. Read the clawback trail for compliance/audit.

DENY ALL HTTP CREATE. Redemption is an internal service call inside the invoice/membership creation transaction via CouponRedeemer::redeem() — never a direct HTTP write from any caller. The policy explicitly denies to prevent accidental exposure.
